signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Options
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.21 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite reports of Iran targeting ships in the Straits of Hormuz. While VIX at 15.7 (normal volatility) keeps carry dominant, the 25d RR at -1.11 (Saxo) signals the options market is paying for downside protection against BoJ intervention risk. JPY COT LF net short -81,624 (week ending May 19) remains a crowded carry trade vulnerable to geopolitical escalation. Carry dominates until VIX > 25.

signals.title: NZD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD/JPY at 94.46 — NZD outperformance (1W avg +0.707%) vs JPY weakness (1W avg -0.363%) creates a 1.070% G8 spread. NZD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkishness) contradicts the RBNZ hike bias (2.25% rate, market bias: Hike). If RBNZ signals further tightening, a short-squeeze in NZD is likely. NZD/JPY carry at +150bp (OIS basis) supports the bilateral divergence.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7832 — IV at 19.5% vs HV30 at 6.1% creates an extreme +13.4% Vol Risk Premium, signaling the options market is pricing in significant tail risk. Retail sentiment is 76% long (Myfxbook), diverging from the COT LF net short -6,015. This institutional vs retail divergence at a 375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage suggests retail is catching a falling knife; further downside targets 0.7750 if US PCE softness persists.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Volatility
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1656 — IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.2% (VRP +2.9%) indicates elevated hedging demand despite a 175bp USD carry advantage. EUR COT LF net short -20,890 (week ending May 19) shows specs are positioned for weakness, but the DXY vs SPX correlation break (z=+1.75σ) suggests USD funding stress is the primary driver. Watch 1.1700 for a break of the bearish trend if US yields continue to soften.
